TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.TRANSL: There was a couple called Sapulungan and Muakai.

S.FORM: ti Muakai a nguanguaq aravats matu qadaw.
S.PHON: ti muakai a ŋuaŋuaq aravaʦ matu qadaw.
S.FORM: ti Muakai a nguanguaq aravac matu qadaw.
S.PHON: ti muakai a ŋuaŋuaq aravaʦ matu qadaw.
S.TRANSL: Muakai was as beautiful as the sun.
But when they slept together,
Muakai had no hole in her private parts.
So Sapulungan was angry.

S.FORM: nu keman tua kinsa, nu temkel tua vava, inika pukaui a kilinguɫ, limutseng sakamaya.
S.PHON: nu kəman tua kinsa, nu təmkəɭ tua vava, inika pukaui a kiɭiŋuʎ, ɭimuʦəŋ sakamaja.
S.FORM: nu keman tua kinsa, nu temkel tua vava, inika pukaui a kilingulj, limuceng sakamaya.
S.PHON: nu kəman tua kinsa, nu təmkəɭ tua vava, inika pukaui a kiɭiŋuʎ, ɭimuʦəŋ sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: When they ate or drank, he refused to sit (in a circle) with the others; he just sulked.

S.FORM: qau muri vaik a qemaɫup ti Sapulungan.
S.PHON: qau muri vaik a qəmaʎup ti sapuɭuŋan.
S.FORM: qau muri vaik a qemaljup ti Sapulungan.
S.PHON: qau muri vaik a qəmaʎup ti sapuɭuŋan.
S.TRANSL: Then Sapulungan was going off hunting,
and had left his pipe in the house.
“Come here, mother, hand me my pipe.”
Muakai took the pipe and gave it to him.
“No, put it back.
Tell my mother to give it to me.”

S.FORM: alapen nua kina sa pavayan tjai Sapulungan.
S.PHON: aɭapən nua kina sa pavajan cai sapuɭuŋan.
S.FORM: alapen nua kina sa pavayan tjai Sapulungan.
S.PHON: aɭapən nua kina sa pavajan ʦai sapuɭuŋan.
S.TRANSL: So his mother took it and gave it to Sapulungan.

S.FORM: "anema nu vavayanan a namaitazua a kutji sakamaya, neka nu buang?"
S.PHON: "anəma nu vavajanan a namaitazua a kuci sakamaja, nəka nu buaŋ"
S.FORM: "anema nu vavayanan a namaitazua a kutji sakamaya, neka nu buang?"
S.PHON: "anəma nu vavajanan a namaitazua a kuʦi sakamaja, nəka nu buaŋ"
S.TRANSL: “What sort of woman is that with no hole in her private parts?”
“No wonder you are angry.”
“Give a drinking party for all the village.
Give the best beer (to her);

S.FORM: nu mapulaw anga, pai patsunu anga."
S.PHON: nu mapuɭaw aŋa, pai paʦunu aŋa."
S.FORM: nu mapulaw anga, pai pacunu anga."
S.PHON: nu mapuɭaw aŋa, pai paʦunu aŋa."
S.TRANSL: and when she is drunk, have a look.”
His mother got the villagers to make beer.

S.FORM: ka makesanga a vava, patsunin a tjaɫasanguaqan a vava, sa "tekeli kati Muakai," aya, sa tekelen na maḍusa.
S.PHON: ka makəsaŋa a vava, paʦunin a caʎasaŋuaqan a vava, sa "təkəɭi kati muakai," aja, sa təkəɭən na maɖusa.
S.FORM: ka makesanga a vava, pacunin a tjaljasanguaqan a vava, sa "tekeli kati Muakai," aya, sa tekelen na madrusa.
S.PHON: ka makəsaŋa a vava, paʦunin a ʦaʎasaŋuaqan a vava, sa "təkəɭi kati muakai," aja, sa təkəɭən na maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: When the beer was ready, she found the best beer, and said: “Muakai, let’s drink together,” and the two of them drank it (or “Let’s drink with Muakai;” and they drank with her).

S.FORM: ka mapulaw anga ti Muakai, seqereng a sema surur.
S.PHON: ka mapuɭaw aŋa ti muakai, səqərəŋ a səma surur.
S.FORM: ka mapulaw anga ti Muakai, seqereng a sema surur.
S.PHON: ka mapuɭaw aŋa ti muakai, səqərəŋ a səma surur.
S.TRANSL: When Muakai was drunk, she lay down on the bed platform.

S.FORM: izua qaliqali a maisuisu i tsasaw.
S.PHON: izua qaɭiqaɭi a maisuisu i ʦasaw.
S.FORM: izua qaliqali a maisuisu i casaw.
S.PHON: izua qaɭiqaɭi a maisuisu i ʦasaw.
S.TRANSL: There was someone pounding grain outside.
“I’ll close the window, friend,

S.FORM: mana nu malavu ti su ḍava."
S.PHON: mana nu maɭavu ti su ɖava."
S.FORM: mana nu malavu ti su drava."
S.PHON: mana nu maɭavu ti su ɖava."
S.TRANSL: in case the chaff goes on your friend.”
She undid Muakai’s clothes.
“What my son says is quite right.

S.FORM: manu namaitutsu, a neka nu buang."
S.PHON: manu namaituʦu, a nəka nu buaŋ."
S.FORM: manu namaitucu, a neka nu buang."
S.PHON: manu namaituʦu, a nəka nu buaŋ."
S.TRANSL: So it’s like that; there’s no hole.”

S.FORM: azua buang matu tsalinga nua ɫigim.
S.PHON: azua buaŋ matu ʦaɭiŋa nua ʎigim.
S.FORM: azua buang matu calinga nua ljigim.
S.PHON: azua buaŋ matu ʦaɭiŋa nua ʎigim.
S.TRANSL: The hole was like the eye of a needle.

S.FORM: malap ta siqunu a kina, sa tsukisen a seman buang.
S.PHON: maɭap ta siqunu a kina, sa ʦukisən a səman buaŋ.
S.FORM: malap ta siqunu a kina, sa cukisen a seman buang.
S.PHON: maɭap ta siqunu a kina, sa ʦukisən a səman buaŋ.
S.TRANSL: His mother took a knife and cut a hole.

S.FORM: ka mapapaulingaw, "alapu tua zaɫum, sa su pavanaw."
S.PHON: ka mapapauɭiŋaw, "aɭapu tua zaʎum, sa su pavanaw."
S.FORM: ka mapapaulingaw, "alapu tua zaljum, sa su pavanaw."
S.PHON: ka mapapauɭiŋaw, "aɭapu tua zaʎum, sa su pavanaw."
S.TRANSL: When Muakai was sober, his mother said: “Get some water and bathe yourself.”

S.FORM: malap tua zaɫum ti Muakai, sa pavanaw.
S.PHON: maɭap tua zaʎum ti muakai, sa pavanaw.
S.FORM: malap tua zaljum ti Muakai, sa pavanaw.
S.PHON: maɭap tua zaʎum ti muakai, sa pavanaw.
S.TRANSL: Muakai got some water, and bathed herself.

S.FORM: ka mavanaw anga, samaɫi anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: ka mavanaw aŋa, samaʎi aŋa sakamaja.
S.FORM: ka mavanaw anga, samalji anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: ka mavanaw aŋa, samaʎi aŋa sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: When she had bathed, she was very surprised.
“Why is it like this?
There’s a hole now in my private parts.”
His mother looked.
“What’s up with you, Muakai?
Why are you behaving like that?”
“Well, look at my private parts.”

S.FORM: "kana vavayan namaitutsu a mapuɫat; inika amin sun a namaitazua.
S.PHON: "kana vavajan namaituʦu a mapuʎat; inika amin sun a namaitazua.
S.FORM: "kana vavayan namaitucu a mapuljat; inika amin sun a namaitazua.
S.PHON: "kana vavajan namaituʦu a mapuʎat; inika amin sun a namaitazua.
S.TRANSL: “Every woman is like that. It’s not only you.

S.FORM: mana sika limutsemutseng ni Sapulungan tjanusun."
S.PHON: mana sika ɭimuʦəmuʦəŋ ni sapuɭuŋan canusun."
S.FORM: mana sika limucemuceng ni Sapulungan tjanusun."
S.PHON: mana sika ɭimuʦəmuʦəŋ ni sapuɭuŋan ʦanusun."
S.TRANSL: That’s why Sapulungan was angry with you.”
“Oh, mother, so that was it.”

S.FORM: ka mangtjengtjez anga ti Sapulungan, vaik a kina a kitsevungan a mapedjelidjeli.
S.PHON: ka maŋcəŋcəz aŋa ti sapuɭuŋan, vaik a kina a kiʦəvuŋan a mapəɉəɭiɉəɭi.
S.FORM: ka mangtjengtjez anga ti Sapulungan, vaik a kina a kicevungan a mapedjelidjeli.
S.PHON: ka maŋʦəŋʦəz aŋa ti sapuɭuŋan, vaik a kina a kiʦəvuŋan a mapəɉəɭiɉəɭi.
S.TRANSL: When Sapulungan came back, his mother went to meet him laughing.

S.FORM: mintuluq ti Sapulungan, temavikiɫ tjai Muakai, sana ivulungan, sana puaɫak.
S.PHON: mintuɭuq ti sapuɭuŋan, təmavikiʎ cai muakai, sana ivuɭuŋan, sana puaʎak.
S.FORM: mintuluq ti Sapulungan, temavikilj tjai Muakai, sana ivulungan, sana pualjak.
S.PHON: mintuɭuq ti sapuɭuŋan, təmavikiʎ ʦai muakai, sana ivuɭuŋan, sana puaʎak.
S.TRANSL: Sapulungan ran and embraced Muakai; they had intercourse; and had a child.
And they all lived happily ever after.
The end.
